title: Custom callout node (info, warning, tip variants)
description: Build a custom Tiptap node extension for `callout`, rendering a styled box with a variant (`info | warning | tip`), containing rich text content, insertable and switchable via the toolbar.
acceptanceCriteria: A custom Tiptap `Node` extension `callout` is registered, allowing nested rich-text content inside it (at least paragraphs, bold/italic)
Callout has a `variant` attribute constrained to `info | warning | tip`, stored in the node's attrs
Toolbar provides an 'insert callout' action with a variant picker (defaults to `info`)
An existing callout's variant can be changed after insertion (e.g. via a small in-editor control on the callout itself)
Each variant renders with a distinct visual style (icon/color) matching the variant
`editor.getJSON()` output for a callout matches the `CalloutBlockContent` shape defined in TASK-3

steps.details: Create `apps/admin/src/features/editor/nodes/Callout.ts` extending `Node.create({ name: 'callout', group: 'block', content: 'block+', ... })` with an `variant` attribute (default `'info'`) parsed/rendered via `data-variant`.

steps.description: Build the NodeView for visual rendering
steps.details: Implement a React NodeView (`ReactNodeViewRenderer`) rendering a bordered/colored box with an icon matching `variant`, and a `<NodeViewContent />` slot for nested rich text.

steps.description: Add insert-callout toolbar action with variant picker
steps.details: Add a toolbar dropdown/button group to insert a callout node with a chosen variant, calling `editor.chain().focus().insertContent({ type: 'callout', attrs: { variant }, content: [...] }).run()`.

In the NodeView, add small variant-selector controls (e.g. three icon buttons) that update the node's `variant` attribute via `updateAttributes({ variant })` without recreating the node or losing its content.

Confirm the callout's `editor.getJSON()` node shape (`{ type: 'callout', attrs: { variant }, content: [...] }`) matches `CalloutBlockContent` in `packages/schema/src/block.ts`; adjust the schema type if needed.
Using Playwright: insert a warning callout, type text inside it, switch it to tip, and confirm the visual style updates and the content is preserved.

technicalNotes: This is the first custom node in the editor — establish the NodeView/React pattern cleanly here since `component-embed` (TASK-29, TASK-47) will follow the same structure.
